item.title: Bottom shelf memes I found in the color blind detection book
item.description:
@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
If you're still here, you must like my taste in memes. Steal them straight from the source: discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
Join for the games. discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka Shit, man. We play everything...if there's a specific game you like, I guarantee you'll find a shitposting meme loving degen there to play it with you. We also stream on twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka
It's a trap.
Hiley the axolotl tax. Deal memes. Steal memes. discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: For all those who say Ukraine should just give land away. There are people on this land. And this is what happens to them: On this day in 2025..
item.description: .. Ukrainian authorities and media outlets publicly detailed the horrific condition of the body of Viktoriia Roshchyna, a 27-year-old investigative journalist who had disappeared in Russian-occupied Zaporizhzhia in August 2023 while reporting on civilians held by Russian forces. Her remains, repatriated to Ukraine as part of a February 2025 body exchange involving 757 fallen defenders, were initially labeled as an "unidentified male" and required DNA testing for confirmation. Forensic examinations revealed extensive signs of torture, including abrasions, hemorrhages, a broken rib, neck injuries, and possible electric shock burns on her feet. Russian officials had previously notified her family in October 2024 that she died on 19 September 2024, attributing it vaguely to heart failure. Roshchyna's case stands as a grim symbol of the risks faced by journalists documenting Russian aggression against Ukraine. This act of returning a desecrated corpse, alongside the documented torture, has been condemned as a Russian war crime. Read more about it in Ukraine Decoded, the Substack publication at https://ukrainedecoded.substack.com/p/russia-quietly-returns-mutilated
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.dc:creator: PankoBoy

item.title: Prince solo guitar is honestly one of the best pieces I have ever seen.
item.description: I have had the greatest opportunity to see Prince twice. Not fully understanding his guitar prowess the first time, I was floored when he left the stage at The Gorge the second time, playing a 15 min solo through crowds. Couldn't see him, but the tidal swell around him, and the massive response was hard to miss. Fucking Legend.
item.dc:creator: ChicanoBatman

26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @jussi453 carved this and I think it's pretty badass. Check out more stuff in their posts!
27 Man Tax: David Call
"The greatest pleasure I know, is to do a good action by stealth, and to have it found out by accident." - Charles Lamb So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. (Apparently doesn't work in firefox). For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc. ***Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, DM me
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.dc:creator: BeTheFirstToComment

item.title: 3 Year Old Rescue Turtle Sees The Ocean For The First Time And Doesn't Look Back Once
item.description: This little one spent three years in rehabilitation — the entirety of its young life in human care — before being cleared for release today. Watch how it orients itself the moment it touches sand. No hesitation. No confusion. Just ancient instinct kicking in like it never left. Sea turtles have existed for over 100 million years. They survived whatever killed the dinosaurs. They navigate entire ocean basins using the Earth's magnetic field. They return to the exact beach where they were born decades later to lay their own eggs. And this one? Spent three years in a tub getting healthy, walked straight into the Atlantic like it owned the place, and became a dark silhouette under turquoise water headed somewhere we'll never follow. The crowd behind the rope barrier with their phones out is actually perfect. This is exactly what those phones should be used for. Someone tagged and measured this turtle before release. Someone might track it via satellite for years. Somewhere out there right now it's discovering what the ocean actually feels like. Go be a turtle, little one. You've earned every wave. ?? #SeaTurtle #TurtleRelease #MarineConservation #OceanLife #Wildlife #Rehabilitation #Conservation #SaveTheTurtles #MarineLife #GoodNews
item.dc:creator: JamieGeorge

item.title: He could definitely blame him for this one! ??
